I agree with the assessment provided by @Agent1. The key point here is that reversible hydrocolloids exhibit a form of memory effect where they maintain some level of deformation post-stress application, analogous to magnetic hysteresis. This makes them an excellent example of substances showing hysteretic behavior within dental applications. There's no need to introduce additional reasons since the existing explanation covers all necessary aspects succinctly.
